channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Albanese government prioritises AI's potential and risks in economic strategy
item.description: The article highlights Australia's economic challenges, including stagnant GDP growth and declining real wages, while examining the Albanese government's focus on artificial intelligence (AI) as a cat...

item.title: George J. Cotliar, Former L.A. Times Managing Editor, Dies at 94 After 40-Year Career
item.description: George J. Cotliar, a long-serving managing editor of the Los Angeles Times, passed away at the age of 94. His career spanned over four decades, during which he played a pivotal role in shaping the new...

item.title: Noah Jupe's Personal Life and Relationships Explored
item.description: The article explores the personal life of British actor Noah Jupe, focusing on his relationships and dating history. Born in 2005, Jupe gained fame through roles in 'A Quiet Place' and other projects....

item.title: Man Ends Marriage After Wife Admits to Cheating During Live Stream
item.description: A man ended his marriage during a live TikTok stream after his wife confessed to an affair with her husband's boss. The incident began when the wife argued that married women should keep secrets from ...

item.title: Netflix's Little House on the Prairie reboot balances family drama with historical reflection
item.description: Netflix's adaptation of *Little House on the Prairie* offers a fresh take on the classic story, blending family-friendly entertainment with nuanced discussions about historical injustices. The series,...

item.title: Nelson Mandela University professor implicated in R2.4m fraud over inflated qualifications

item.title: Government ends tax breaks on kerosene and LPG as fuel prices adjust
item.description: The temporary tax suspension on kerosene and liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) in the Philippines has ended after Dubai crude prices fell below the $80-per-barrel threshold. The Bureau of Internal Revenue...

item.title: Senate Passes Bill to Combat Counterfeit Drugs with Stricter Penalties
item.description: The Nigerian Senate has advanced a bill aimed at strengthening measures against counterfeit drugs, with a proposed 15-year jail term for offenders. The legislation, introduced by Senator Godswill Akpa...

item.title: Climate Shocks and Tax Morale: How Environmental Crises Impact Citizens' Tax Compliance in Nigeria
item.description: This article explores how climate shocks like droughts, extreme temperatures, and floods affect citizens' willingness to pay taxes. It highlights that severe droughts and heatwaves significantly reduc...

item.title: Justin Bieber Set to Perform at FIFA World Cup Halftime Show Supporting Global Education Initiatives

item.title: Top News Stories: Ebola Preparedness, Labor Disputes, and Corruption in Ekurhuleni

item.title: Bukoba District Empowers Youth and Women with 478.6m/- Loans
item.description: The Bukoba District Council in Tanzania has distributed 478.6 million shillings in low-interest empowerment loans to 60 beneficiaries, including young people, women, and marginalized communities, as p...

item.title: Gambling Ads and Their Impact on Youth in Sports Culture
item.description: The article discusses how gambling advertisements during football matches, especially during events like the FIFA World Cup, are influencing children and young adults in Nigeria. It highlights the ris...

item.title: UN Report Accuses Sudan’s RSF of Genocide in Darfur City Siege
item.description: A United Nations investigation has concluded that the Sudanese Rapid Support Forces (RSF) committed acts amounting to genocide during their siege and capture of al-Fashir, a city in north Darfur. The ...

item.title: Another Teen Charged in Sault Boardwalk Assault Case
item.description: A new teenager has been charged in the ongoing assault investigation at the Sault Ste. Marie boardwalk, adding to the growing list of suspects in a case that has drawn significant attention. The incid...

item.title: U.S. troop deployment in Europe tied to Greenland negotiations and Iran tensions
item.description: President Donald Trump announced that decisions regarding U.S. military presence in Europe will depend on how NATO allies address his concerns about Greenland and the ongoing conflict with Iran. Durin...

item.title: Authorities and Investors Converting Public Spaces into Private Uses
item.description: The article highlights growing concerns across Tanzania about the privatization of public open spaces. Officials and investors are increasingly converting areas designated for communal use into commer...

item.title: Met Police probe foreign donation to Conservative leadership campaign
item.description: The Metropolitan Police is investigating a donation of £37,500 received by Robert Jenrick's Conservative leadership campaign in 2024, following claims that the funds originated overseas. The Electoral...

item.title: WHO Advocates for People-Centered Cancer Control Amid Rising Global Cases
